population: Adult patients (>18 years) presenting to the emergency department (ED) of a tertiary-care university hospital with an initial diagnosis of sepsis or infection-related conditions and treated with a sepsis protocol.
predictor: National Early Warning Score (NEWS), quick Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (qSOFA), Search Out Severity (SOS) score, Emergency Severity Index (ESI) triage, and Systemic Inflammatory Response System (SIRS) criteria
outcome: Primary: 28-day mortality; Secondary: 28-day mechanical ventilator usage and 28-day vasopressor usage
timing: Predictors measured at ED triage; outcomes measured at 28 days
method: Retrospective cross-sectional study; ROC analysis (AUROC) with 95% CI
performance: NEWS provided the highest AUROC for 28-day mortality (0.770) and outperformed other scores for mechanical ventilator and vasopressor usage prediction.
sensitivity: Reported in Table 3 (not fully visible in text, but NEWS AUROC is 0.770)
specificity: Reported in Table 3 (not fully visible in text, but NEWS AUROC is 0.770)
notes: Evidence extracted from abstract (page 2), methods (page 3), results text, flow chart (Figure 1), and ROC curve (Figure 2) on page 4. The study concludes NEWS is the most accurate triage tool for predicting 28-day mortality in sepsis patients.
